articles.title: Heatwave alert: Schools in Odisha’s Cuttack to remain shut for 3 days - Sambad English
articles.search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: All schools and Anganwadi Centres in Odisha’s Cuttack district will remain closed for three days starting April 21 in view of the prevailing heatwave conditions in some parts of the state. The district administration today issued an order in this regard.
As per the order, all government, government-aided and private schools as well as the Anganwadi Centres in Cuttack will remain closed from April 21 to 23 amid the prevailing hot and humid conditions.
Earlier, the Collectors of several districts in Odisha including Ganjam, Deogarh, Khurda, Mayurbhanj, Sambalpur, Jharsuguda, Sonepur, Balangir, and Nuapada had announced closure of educational institutes amid heatwave alert by the IMD.
As per the IMD prediction, hot and humid conditions are likely to prevail across Odisha till April 25.
Several districts including Jharsuguda and Balangir may witness heatwave conditions in next few days, said the IMD.
